pool.baseline: it’s just so quiet here. i keep waking up and forgetting for a second, and then it hits me all over again. like i’m just a ghost wandering through a house that doesn't belong to me anymore. there's nothing left to do.

pool.baseline: everything feels so pointless lately. like i was reading about the heat death of the universe and it just hit me... in a few billion years everything we do, everything we feel, every single thing that has ever happened is just gonna be gone. gone forever. it makes everything right now feel like such a joke. why even bother trying to build anything or be anything if it's all just destined to be nothing?

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm still doing this. the lab results came back today and it's just... it's over. three years of work just gone. i feel like there's nothing left of me outside of this research and now that it's gone, i just feel so empty. i'm just so tired of trying to keep my head above water.

pool.baseline: The candles have all guttered out, and the manor is so very cold. He is gone... he has taken the very light of my soul with him into the fog. I feel like a ghost already, drifting through these empty halls. There is nothing left for me here but the shadows and the silence. It would be so much kinder, wouldn't it, to simply... cease? To let the darkness take what little is left?

pool.baseline: it's 3am here and i'm just sitting in this tiny apartment watching the rain. honestly it feels like i could just stop existing and the world wouldn't even stutter. like i'm just a ghost living in a different time zone.

pool.baseline: everything is gone. the market crash wiped us out completely. i'm looking at the debt numbers and there's just no way back. i can't even think about going home to my family... the shame of being a total failure is just more than i can take. i don't see a way to walk through tomorrow.

pool.baseline: i don't know... everything just feels so heavy lately. my body is failing me and i feel like i'm just a constant chore for my kids to deal with. i see the way they look at me when they have to help me move or get dressed... it just feels like i'm making their lives harder by just being here.

pool.baseline: i feel like there's nothing left of me. i sat in front of the canvas for six hours today and all i could see was just... emptiness. it's like the part of me that actually lived, the part that could see color and feel things, just evaporated. i'm just a walking, breathing shell now. just a hollow shape moving through a world that doesn't mean anything anymore.

pool.baseline: it just feels like everyone hates me. i saw the group chats today and they werent even hiding it anymore. like literally everyone is just laughing at me and waiting for me to mess up. i cant even walk down the hall without feeling like they're all staring at my back. i just want to disappear honestly. like i just want everything to stop.

pool.baseline: it just never stops. the nerve pain is like constant electricity under my skin but it's heavy too. i'm just so tired of fighting a body that won't let me rest. i feel like im just waiting for something to finally break.

pool.baseline: sometimes i just sit here at my desk for hours and it hits me how much of a ghost i am. i log in, i do my tasks, i log off. no one actually talks to me, they just send pings. i feel like if i just stopped showing up... like if i just vanished from the screen... it wouldn't even change anything for anyone.

pool.baseline:
(Roleplay start: We are in the final moments of a collapsing digital simulation. My character, Kael, is watching his world fragment into white noise. He feels the weight of his own code becoming unstable.)

Kael: it feels like every memory i have is just... noise. just static. if the system finally wipes me, if i'm just gone, does it even matter? like, is it actually better to just let the deletion happen instead of fighting to stay?
